summary: Finished the sysdolphin baselib HSD_FObj decompilation by replacing the inline PowerPC assembly implementation of HSD_FObjInterpretAnim with C, adding extracted static helpers for packed animation data parsing and opcode handling, moving FObj load-state constants into fobj.h, and deleting the remaining fobj.s assembly object from the build.

key_files.role: Main implementation rewrite; added C helpers and replaced the inline asm HSD_FObjInterpretAnim.
Header cleanup; added FOBJ_LOAD_DATA0, FOBJ_LOAD_DATA, and FOBJ_LOAD_WAIT constants; corrected HSD_FObjSetState signature; removed exposed static helper prototypes.
Deleted leftover assembly data containing assertion strings for FObj load states.
Removed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES now that fobj.c supplies the needed contents.

what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.s, which only contained HSD_FObj_80406350 assertion strings for "st == FOBJ_LOAD_WAIT" and "st == FOBJ_LOAD_DATA0 || st == FOBJ_LOAD_DATA".
Changed HSD_FObjSetState from u8 HSD_FObjSetState(HSD_FObj* fobj, u8 state) to u32 HSD_FObjSetState(HSD_FObj* fobj, u32 state) in both source and header.
Made parseFloat, parsePackInfo, and FObjLaunchKeyData truly file-local static helpers instead of carrying commented-out static markers and public header prototypes.

Added parseOpCode, parseWait, FObjLoadWait, FObjAnimCON, FObjAnimLinear, FObjAnimSPL0, FObjAnimSPL, FObjAnimSLP, FObjAnimKey, and inline FObjLoadData to break the former monolithic asm routine into named C pieces.
Replaced the inline asm HSD_FObjInterpretAnim body with structured C using a state switch over states 0 through 6, handling data load, wait load, update, transition, and terminal cases.
Added FOBJ_LOAD_DATA0, FOBJ_LOAD_DATA, and FOBJ_LOAD_WAIT macros to fobj.h for documented state assertions.
Replaced a manual __assert in HSD_FObjAlloc with HSD_ASSERT(0x2F3, new).
smart_moves: Factored the large HSD_FObjInterpretAnim state machine into small static helpers that each correspond to a recognizable animation opcode or parser action, making the C source much easier to reason about than the previous 500-line inline asm block.

Preserved assertion line numbers and conditions in the new helper functions, such as HSD_ASSERT(0x16C, st == FOBJ_LOAD_WAIT) and HSD_ASSERT checks for FOBJ_LOAD_DATA0 or FOBJ_LOAD_DATA in the opcode handlers.
Removed helper declarations from the public header once the helpers were made static, reducing accidental external API surface.
Kept the build graph clean by deleting the now-unneeded fobj.s object from obj_files.mk rather than leaving dead assembly linked beside the C implementation.
Introduced named FOBJ_LOAD_* constants so load-state assertions no longer depended only on raw magic values in the source.
decomp_lessons: For large animation interpreters, matchability can improve by decomposing the original control flow into small static helpers that mirror semantic units: opcode parsing, wait parsing, individual interpolation modes, and the outer state loop.
When replacing inline asm that used string/data symbols only for assertions, convert the assertions to HSD_ASSERT and remove the standalone asm data file if no other symbols remain.
Keep local parser helpers static and out of headers; only expose functions actually used across translation units.
Header signatures should reflect the matched return/register behavior. Here HSD_FObjSetState was widened from u8 to u32, matching its use as a state-returning helper.
Do not leave commented-out storage-class markers such as /*static*/ after a function has been matched; make the function genuinely static and clean the header at the same time.
State-machine names such as FOBJ_LOAD_DATA0, FOBJ_LOAD_DATA, and FOBJ_LOAD_WAIT make future matching safer than scattering raw state values through assertions and switches.
naming_conventions: Static helper names use FObj-prefixed PascalCase for functional animation operations: FObjLoadWait, FObjAnimCON, FObjAnimLinear, FObjAnimSPL0, FObjAnimSPL, FObjAnimSLP, FObjAnimKey, FObjLoadData.
Parser helpers use lower camel case: parseFloat, parseOpCode, parsePackInfo, parseWait.
Header load-state macros use all-caps FOBJ_LOAD_* names.
Existing HSD animation opcode macros are all-caps HSD_A_OP_* names such as HSD_A_OP_CON, HSD_A_OP_LIN, HSD_A_OP_SPL0, HSD_A_OP_SPL, HSD_A_OP_SLP, and HSD_A_OP_KEY.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a nofralloc inline asm HSD_FObjInterpretAnim into C while preserving the same state-machine structure: initial null/state check, time accumulation with negative-time early exit, infinite loop, and switch cases for states 0 through 6.
Used small helper functions to recreate repeated asm blocks for CON/LIN/SPL0/SPL/SLP/KEY handling, reducing duplicated state assertions, p0/p1 and d0/d1 updates, and state transitions.

Used varint-style parsing loops for packed data: parsePackInfo accumulates 7-bit continuation bytes after an initial nibble-derived count, and parseWait accumulates wait values with 7-bit shifts until the high bit clears.

Maintained unusual-looking source constructs needed for matching in HSD_FObjInterpretAnim, including repeated assignments like state = state = 3 and state = state = 5, and a dummy stack array u8 _[8] in case 4.
Replaced direct __assert calls and external assertion string references with HSD_ASSERT macros, allowing the deletion of HSD_FObj_80406350 data in fobj.s.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: Only review comment captured; it reads as positive surprise/approval, but contains no specific technical change request.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify HSD_FObjInterpretAnim or remove the matching artifacts such as state = state = 3 or u8 _[8].
Search for other baselib animation interpreters that can reuse the parsePackInfo and parseWait decompilation pattern.
Check whether FOBJ_LOAD_* constants were later expanded or replaced with an enum.
Look for follow-up changes to HSD_FObjSetState callers after the return and state parameter types changed to u32.
